joke.raw_joke: My son was just born — My son was just born and another dad at the nursery congratulated me and said his daughter was born yesterday... said maybe they'll marry each other. Sure, like my son is going to marry someone twice his age...

blocks.content:
RILEY_CASTING_PLAN_v1

Primary Cast (2-3 roles):
1. role_needed: New dad narrator
   selected_cast_persona: jerry · coach-dad
   why_this_fit: Warm dad energy and clear comedic timing.
2. role_needed: Other nursery dad
   selected_cast_persona: noah · dad
   why_this_fit: Grounded secondary dad for setup handoff.
3. role_needed: Nurse / staff (optional)
   selected_cast_persona: iris · startup-founder
   why_this_fit: Professional look without distracting costume cues.

Adaptation Notes:
1. Hospital/nursery backdrop; no medical gore detail.
2. Keep babies implied (off-screen bassinet or blanket bundle).

Shot Intent:
- setup_shot: Setup: two dads chatting in nursery hallway.
- punchline_reaction_shot: Punchline: narrator side-eye reaction close-up after "twice his age" line.

---

IRIS_VIDEO_PROMPT_v2

seed_id: seed-002
aspect_ratio: 9:16 (vertical portrait)
concept_count: 3

Concept A — Performance-First Character Comedy
duration_target: 9.8s
duration_reason: Dialogue requires two dad beats plus reaction button. Keep straightforward continuity cuts for readability.
master_video_prompt:
Hospital nursery hallway cartoon, cast jerry coach-dad + noah dad (+ optional iris startup-founder nurse cameo), family-safe tone, soft pastel medical setting, clear facial acting, 9:16 framing, strong caption-safe regions, quick conversational cuts. Prioritize facial acting and clean two-shot/close-up rhythm; minimal background motion.
scene_plan:
1) Scene 1 (0.0–1.6s): Hook title beat “My son was just born,” narrator proud smile.
2) Scene 2 (1.6–4.7s): Other dad congratulates and says kids might marry, over-shoulder shot swap.
3) Scene 3 (4.7–7.5s): Narrator processes line, slight pause, eye-shift reaction.
4) Scene 4 (7.5–9.8s): Punchline close-up: “Like my son is gonna marry someone twice his age…” with deadpan stare.
caption_plan:
1. 0.1s: “My son was just born.”
2. 1.8s: “Another dad said maybe our kids will marry someday…”
3. 5.1s: “Sure…”
4. 7.7s: “Like my son is gonna marry someone twice his age.”
performance_notes:
- Conversational speed, micro-pause before “Sure…”, punchline with dry incredulity. Balanced pace, clear pause before punchline.
risk_and_fallback:
1. Risk: newborn visuals distracting → keep babies implied/off-screen.
2. Fallback: use nursery sign + two-dad dialogue only.
why_jerry_might_greenlight_this:
- Most reliable laugh-read and easiest to produce under budget/time pressure.
vince_handoff:
- aspectRatio: 9:16
- durationSec: 9.8
- sceneCount: 4
- mustKeep: Scene 2 proposal line + Scene 4 age punchline
- optionalTrims: Compress Scene 1 to 1.0s if under 9s target needed
